source_title: DOW-UAP-D58, Range Fouler Debrief, NA, October 2020
description_blurb:
This document is a Range Fouler Debrief, a standardized reporting form the U.S. Navy uses to record the circumstances surrounding an unauthorized intrusion into controlled airspace during active military operations or training. These reports contain a narrative description of the observer’s experiences. 
A U.S. military operator reported an encounter with a group of two UAP. The operator described the UAP as “balloon-shaped,” metallic, and reflective, characterizing them as “2x red blinking strobes.” The report states that “one range fouler was circling around the other.”
All descriptive and estimative language contained in this report reflects the reporter’s subjective interpretation at the time of the event. Such characterizations should not be interpreted as a conclusive indication of the presence or absence of any intrinsic object features or performance characteristics.
title: DOW-UAP-D58, Range Fouler Debrief, NA, October 2020
summary: A U.S. military operator reported an encounter with a group of two UAP during a DCA mission at night. The operator obtained radar lock and target pod video, showing two IR significant contacts. The UAP were described as "balloon-shaped," metallic, and reflective, characterized by "2x red blinking strobes." One UAP was observed circling the other. The objects disappeared in 1/30th of a second, and noise jamming was received, indicated by two chevrons.
reported_object_description: Balloon-shaped, metallic, opaque, reflective, with 2x red blinking strobes.
reported_behavior: Moving (060/20), one circling the other, disappeared in 1/30th of a second, caused noise jamming.
bust_reasoning: The bust score is low due to multiple highly anomalous features reported across multiple sensor modalities. The object was detected by radar, target pod video (IR), visual observation ('Tally Achieved' with '2x red blinking strobes'), and electronic warfare indications ('noise jamming'). The most significant feature resisting mundane explanation is the reported kinematics: the objects 'disappeared in 1/30th of a second,' which is a sub-second disappearance incompatible with known conventional craft. Additionally, the objects exhibited controlled behavior ('one range fouler was circling around the other') and emitted active electronic signals ('noise jamming'). While described as 'balloon-shaped,' this single descriptive element is heavily contradicted by the observed behavior and multi-sensor data, pushing the score into the 'behavior incompatible with known craft' category.
full_text:
# DOW-UAP-D58, Range Fouler Debrief, NA, October 2020 - Page 1

[STAMP: Declassified by MG Richard A. Harrison USCENTCOM Chief of Staff Declassified on: 20 March 2026]

~~SECRET//NOFORN~~

# Range Fouler Debrief Form

Please do not use the purple "submit" button above. Save and email the complete file manually (see below).

Please complete this form to the best of your ability. If you do not have the requested information, please leave the field blank. If there was more than one "group," please report each on a separate form for data collection purposes. You should receive a response within 5 business days.

| Last Name, First Name | Rank | Squadron | SIPR Email Address | Crew Position |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| | O-3 | 77 EFS | | |

This information is for contact only. SPEAR sanitizes all reports of identifying information. Absolutely no identifying information for aircrew or squadron will be recorded for analysis.

| Date (mm/dd/yy) | Time of detection (hh:mm:ss Z) | Day / Night | Side No. | Buno | Mission Description (CAS, BFM, etc) | LFE? |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| 10/27/20 | 01:12:21 | Night | | | DCA | No |

| Contact Working Area (Ex: W-72 1A) | Contact Latitude (DD:MM:SS) | N/S | Contact Longitude (DD:MM:SS) | E/W | Contact Altitude (Ex: 22000) | Altitude Constant? | Wind Dir at Contact Alt (From) | Wind Speed |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| | | | | | 26000 | No | | |

Please attempt to determine a Lat / Long for the contact at initial detection. We recommend using JMPS to reference the sensor-derived bearing and range relative to the bullseye you had in use. If that method is not available, any reasonable derivation of location will work, but please be as specific as possible and make a note in the comments section. These locations may be used to cue other means of tracking.

| Was the contact moving? | Direction/Speed (Ex: 090/15) |
| :--- | :--- |
| Yes | 060/20 |

**Radar Equipped:** Other
**Stable Trackfile?:** Yes
**# of Contacts in "Group":** 2

**AIM-9x Self-Track:** [ ]
**ATFLIR Autotrack:** [ ]
**Tally Achieved:** [x]

**EA Indications (Check all that apply):**
[ ] ECM Arc
[ ] Letter Identifier
[ ] False Trackfiles
[x] Other/Ambiguous

**Please check all that apply:**
[ ] Round
[ ] Square
[x] Balloon-shaped
[ ] Wings/Airframe
[x] Other Shape
[ ] Apparent Propulsion
[ ] Moving Parts
[x] Metallic
[ ] Markings
[ ] Translucent
[x] Opaque
[x] Reflective

Please use the field below to describe the contact and any interaction in your own words with as much detail as possible. Please be sure to include any detail not included in questions above.

KINGPIN DIRECTED ID OF UNKNOWN CONTACT. 1.4a OBTAINED RADAR LOCK AND TARGET POD VIDEO BUT UNABLE TO GET CLOSER THAN 16.9NM TO GET A BETTER ID. THE TARGET POD SHOWED 2 IR SIGNIFICANT CONTACTS. ONE RANGE FOULER WAS CIRCLING AROUND THE OTHER. IN 1/30TH OF A SECOND, THEY WERE GONE. TALLY ACHIEVED WAS 2X RED BLINKING STROBES AND NOISE JAMMING WAS RECIEVED. NOISE JAMMING WAS INDICATED BY TWO CHEVRONS.

FIRST DETECTION OF THE RANGE FOULERS WERE AT B/E ZIM 248/17 1.4a

Don't use the purple "submit" button! Save this form with filename "Date_Squadron_RF.pdf" and email it to [redacted] (Also in the global). For troubleshooting, call [redacted]

Thank you for your time. Please ensure all display tapes are ripped for the entire time of interaction and saved as a .wmv (Example: 4 May VFA-106 HUD.wmv). Squadron intel personnel shall upload those files to the repository located at this link. [redacted]

~~SECRET//NOFORN~~

USCENTCOM MDR 26-0038 to MDR 26-0046 Approved for Release to AARO 03/27/26 000001
cover_up_reasoning: The document shows routine redaction consistent with standard military operational security and privacy protocols. Identifying information for the operator, squadron, specific contact coordinates, and internal repository links are redacted, which is explicitly stated as part of the reporting process ('SPEAR sanitizes all reports of identifying information'). The document is marked 'SECRET//NOFORN' but also bears a declassification stamp and indicates approval for release to AARO, suggesting a process of controlled transparency rather than active concealment of the event details. There are no references to classified annexes or extensive black-bar redactions obscuring the narrative.
title_zh: DOW-UAP-D58，靶场干扰物情况汇报，NA，2020年10月
summary_zh: 一名美国军事操作员报告，在夜间执行DCA任务期间遭遇两架UAP。该操作员获取了雷达锁定和目标吊舱视频，显示两个红外特征显著的目标。这些UAP被描述为“气球状”、金属质地且具有反射性，特征为“2个红色闪烁频闪灯”。观察到其中一个UAP围绕另一个UAP盘旋。这些物体在1/30秒内消失，并接收到噪声干扰，由两个V形符号指示。
reported_object_description_zh: 气球状，金属质地，不透明，具有反射性，带有2个红色闪烁频闪灯。
reported_behavior_zh: 移动中 (060/20)，其中一个围绕另一个盘旋，在1/30秒内消失，造成噪声干扰。
bust_reasoning_zh: 排除得分较低，因为通过多种传感器模式报告了多项高度异常的特征。该物体通过雷达、目标吊舱视频 (IR)、目视观察（“已确认目标”并带有“2个红色闪烁频闪灯”）以及电子战迹象（“噪声干扰”）被探测到。最显著的、难以用常规解释的特征是报告的运动学：物体“在1/30秒内消失”，这种亚秒级消失与已知常规飞行器不符。此外，这些物体表现出受控行为（“一个靶场干扰物围绕另一个盘旋”）并发出主动电子信号（“噪声干扰”）。尽管被描述为“气球状”，但这一单一描述元素与观察到的行为和多传感器数据严重矛盾，使得该事件被归入“行为与已知飞行器不符”的类别。
cover_up_reasoning_zh: 该文件显示了符合标准军事行动安全和隐私协议的常规编辑。操作员、中队、具体接触坐标和内部存储库链接的识别信息均被编辑，这在报告流程中明确说明（“SPEAR对所有报告中的识别信息进行清理”）。该文件标有“SECRET//NOFORN”字样，但也带有解密印章并表明已获准发布给AARO，这表明是一种受控的透明化过程，而非主动隐瞒事件细节。没有提及机密附件或大量黑条编辑以掩盖叙述。
claims: UAP described as balloon-shaped, metallic, and reflective
UAP characterized by 2x red blinking strobes
One UAP observed circling the other
Radar lock and target pod video obtained
UAP disappeared in 1/30th of a second
claims_zh: UAP被描述为气球状、金属质地且具有反射性
UAP特征为2个红色闪烁频闪灯
观察到其中一个UAP围绕另一个盘旋
获取了雷达锁定和目标吊舱视频
bust_explanations: Highly advanced, experimental military drone or countermeasure: The 'balloon-shaped' description could be a misidentification or a specific design feature. The controlled circling behavior, active noise jamming, and especially the instantaneous disappearance ('in 1/30th of a second') could be features of a highly classified system designed for evasion, electronic warfare, or rapid self-destruction/cloaking.

Unidentified high-altitude balloon with advanced electronic warfare capabilities and a rapid self-destruct/descent mechanism: This explanation attempts to reconcile the 'balloon-shaped' description with the anomalous behavior. The strobes and noise jamming would be active components, and the instantaneous disappearance would require a mechanism for rapid destruction or descent that makes it vanish from all sensors simultaneously, which is highly speculative.

Complex sensor malfunction or environmental interference: While multi-sensor data is reported, a highly specific and simultaneous malfunction across radar, IR, and visual (for the disappearance) combined with a misinterpretation of EW indications could theoretically create this scenario. However, the specific quantification of the disappearance time and the convergent observations make this highly improbable.
bust_explanations_zh: 高度先进的实验性军用无人机或对抗措施：“气球状”的描述可能是一种误认或特定的设计特征。受控的盘旋行为、主动噪声干扰，尤其是瞬间消失（“在1/30秒内”）可能是为规避、电子战或快速自毁/隐形而设计的高度机密系统的特征。
具有先进电子战能力和快速自毁/下降机制的未知高空探测气球：这种解释试图将“气球状”的描述与异常行为相协调。频闪灯和噪声干扰将是主动组件，而瞬间消失则需要一种使其同时从所有传感器中消失的快速销毁或下降机制，这具有高度推测性。
复杂的传感器故障或环境干扰：尽管报告了多传感器数据，但雷达、IR和视觉（针对消失）的高度特定且同时发生的故障，加上对电子战迹象的误解，理论上可能造成这种情况。然而，对消失时间的具体量化和趋同的观察结果使得这种情况极不可能。
cover_up_indicators: Routine redaction of PII (names, squadron, email, phone)
Redaction of operational details (specific coordinates, internal repository link)
Document marked 'SECRET//NOFORN' but subsequently declassified and approved for release to AARO
cover_up_indicators_zh: 例行编辑个人身份信息（姓名、中队、电子邮件、电话）
编辑行动细节（具体坐标、内部存储库链接）
文件标有“SECRET//NOFORN”字样，但随后被解密并获准发布给AARO
